intro: Planning your baby's nutrition requires careful attention to health, development, and feeding schedules. From newborn through 6+ months, each stage has different formula requirements and considerations. This calculator helps determine the daily ml of infant formula needed based on your baby's age and weight. All values are current for 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **ml/day = weight in kg × 150 approximately**. Direct calculation using your baby's information.

fields.label: Weight in kg
fields.help: Baby's weight in kilograms. Weigh without clothing.

explanation:
**Infant nutrition and feeding** involve health, development, and consistency. Each stage has different formula requirements and feeding schedules.

## How it's calculated

This calculator uses the standard infant formula guideline of approximately 150 ml per kilogram of body weight per day for babies up to 6 months old. It checks your inputs against typical ranges and alerts you if values fall outside expected guidelines.

## Important notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For medical or nutritional decisions, consult with a pediatrician or healthcare professional. Values are current for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How many ml per day for a 3-month-old baby?
faq.a: A 3-month-old typically needs 150 ml/kg/day. For a 6 kg baby, that's approximately 900 ml daily.

faq.q: How many times a day should a baby be fed?
faq.a: At 1 month: 8 times. At 3 months: 6-7 times. At 6 months: 5 times daily.

faq.q: Can I combine breast milk and formula?
faq.a: Yes, combination feeding is common. Breastfeed on demand and supplement with formula as needed.

faq.q: How should I prepare infant formula safely?
faq.a: Use water heated to 70°C (158°F) to eliminate harmful bacteria. Always follow the formula package instructions.

faq.q: When should I start introducing solid foods?
faq.a: Most babies are ready around 6 months. Watch for signs of readiness like sitting up and losing the tongue-thrust reflex.

faq.q: What formula stage should I use for my baby's age?
faq.a: Stage 1 for newborns, Stage 2 around 6 months, Stage 3 from 12 months. Always check your pediatrician's recommendation.

faq.q: How long can prepared formula sit at room temperature?
faq.a: No more than 2 hours. Discard any unused formula after this time to prevent bacterial growth.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes, all our calculators are free and don't require registration.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
